name: Money Mike Pawn Shop
description_short: NYC pawn shop in Harlem specializing in luxury resale: designer handbags, high-end watches, limited-edition sneakers, gold, diamonds, and rare collectibles.
description_long:
Money Mike Pawn Shop is an independent pawn and luxury resale shop located at 1711 Amsterdam Ave in the Harlem neighborhood of Manhattan, New York City. Unlike traditional pawn shops that deal primarily in consumer electronics and tools, Money Mike has carved out a niche in the high-end resale market, catering to customers looking to pawn or sell designer and luxury goods. The shop's founding year is unverified, and it operates as a small independent business with no national chain affiliations or published certifications.

The shop's core offering covers buy, sell, and pawn transactions across a range of luxury and collectible categories. Customers can bring in fine jewelry and diamonds, luxury watches, designer handbags, limited-edition sneakers, gold and silver, antiques, vintage items, and rare memorabilia and collectibles. The business also markets expert appraisal services and emphasizes a no-hidden-fees approach to valuation — a claim that appears prominently in its customer-facing messaging. No formal loan amounts, interest rates, or fee schedules are published online, which is typical for pawn operations where terms are set per transaction.

Money Mike stands out within the local pawn shop landscape for its deliberate focus on the luxury segment, a relatively uncommon specialization in a market dominated by generalist shops. The business holds a 5.0/5 Google rating from 160 reviews — an unusually high score that suggests consistent customer satisfaction. It also maintains an active Instagram presence at @moneymikepawnshop, which appears to serve as its primary marketing channel for showcasing inventory and attracting buyers of high-end resale goods.

The shop's strengths are its luxury niche, strong reputation, and accessible Harlem location. However, there are real limitations to note. Pawn loan rates, fees, and terms are not disclosed publicly, so consumers cannot comparison-shop without visiting in person. The shop is closed on Saturdays, which is an unusual gap for a retail pawn operation. No BBB profile or third-party accreditation was found, and the use of a Gmail address as the primary business contact reflects the shop's small independent scale. Buyers and borrowers should call ahead to confirm current inventory and loan terms.
pros: Perfect 5.0/5 Google rating from 160 reviews — unusually high score for a pawn shop
Specializes in luxury and designer goods, a rare niche not common among NYC pawn shops
Wide category coverage: designer handbags, luxury watches, limited-edition sneakers, gold, diamonds, antiques, and memorabilia
Expert appraisals marketed as transparent with no hidden fees
Convenient Upper Manhattan location at 1711 Amsterdam Ave in Harlem
Open seven days a week (Mon–Fri and Sunday 10am–7pm) with extended hours
Active Instagram presence (@moneymikepawnshop) for browsing available inventory
cons: No published pawn loan rates, interest rates, or fee schedule — terms are only available in person
Closed on Saturdays, an unusual gap for a retail pawn and resale operation
No BBB accreditation or rating found, limiting third-party accountability verification
Primary contact is a Gmail address, reflecting limited formal business infrastructure
No mobile app or online pawn/loan portal for remote transactions
best_for: NYC residents looking to pawn or sell luxury watches, designer handbags, or fine jewelry
Collectors seeking to sell rare memorabilia, vintage items, or limited-edition sneakers
Harlem and Upper Manhattan residents needing quick collateral-based cash on high-value items
Buyers shopping for pre-owned luxury goods at resale prices in New York City
diagnosis: Money Mike Pawn Shop is genuinely best suited for New York City residents who need to quickly convert luxury or designer goods into cash, or who are hunting for high-end resale deals in Harlem. Its 5.0 Google rating from 160 customers is a credible signal of consistent service quality in a competitive urban market. The main caveat is that pawn loan rates and fee structures are entirely unpublished, so consumers should contact the shop directly before making any assumptions about loan terms or valuations.
services: Pawn loans on luxury and high-value goods
Buy and sell gold, silver, and diamonds
Buy and sell antiques and vintage items
Buy and sell rare memorabilia and collectibles
